failingTest: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns undefined instead of the prosody definition (case-sensitive comparison bug)
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — normalize tag name comparison to lowercase in getTagDef()
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add uppercase and mixed-case test cases for getTagDef and isTagSupportedByProvider
fixApproach: In `apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts`, change `getTagDef` from `SSML_TAGS.find((t) => t.tag === tagName)` to `SSML_TAGS.find((t) => t.tag === tagName.toLowerCase())`. This single-line change fixes both `getTagDef` and the transitive `isTagSupportedByProvider`. Then add 5 new test cases in `tags.test.ts`: uppercase lookup, mixed-case lookup, confirming lowercase still works, nonexistent still returns undefined, and that `isTagSupportedByProvider` returns the same result for uppercase and lowercase variants of the same tag.
steps: Modify `getTagDef()` in `apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts`: change `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag === tagName.toLowerCase()`

Add test cases in `apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts`: uppercase (`'PROSODY'`), mixed-case (`'Break'`), confirm lowercase still works (`'emphasis'`), confirm nonexistent still returns undefined, and assert `isTagSupportedByProvider('PROSODY', p)` equals `isTagSupportedByProvider('prosody', p)`
Run `npm run test` to verify all existing and new tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive by normalizing both sides to lowercase, fixing SSML tag lookup for uppercase/mixed-case tag names.
